$comment: The authority signed-core bytes ARE canon-core v2 `canonical_bytes(core)`. This contract carries a metadata-safe-ASCII / integer-only PORT of that one normative algorithm (the AUTHORITY PROFILE — non-ASCII/float/null are fail-closed rejected by design, amendment #3), NOT a fourth canon. These digests pin the exact spec canon-core v2 source the port was proven byte-identical against; a change here must re-verify parity.

authority_profile.field_constraints: every signed-core string is metadata-safe ASCII (0x21-0x7E minus '"' and '\\'); no floats/NaN/Inf; no null; ASCII snake_case keys
authority_profile.signature: ed25519 over the canonical bytes above
authority_profile.domain_separation_status: OPEN COORDINATION (XG-4): canon-core v2 has no `authority` digest profile yet (profiles: semantic-content, run, artifact-byte, certificate). The signature currently covers BARE canonical_bytes(core). If the fleet decides the authority signature must cover the future authority-profile digest preimage (spec-canon:v2:authority\n || canonical_bytes(core)), every signature regenerates — CONFIRM before Portal builds the real signer (Slice 2).
